title: CPU-Base Bookshelf with Drawer
summary: A short plywood cabinet sized to sit under a desk with a computer tower, topped by a shared desk surface and holding one drawer plus open cubby storage.
description:
This is a desk-system base, meant to sit under a continuous countertop that spans a whole desk run rather than carrying its own standalone top. Build the base to that in mind — the divider splits the interior into an open cubby (tall enough for a desktop tower) and a drawer bay, with 1x2s across the top tying the case together and giving the shared countertop something to screw into.

Size the drawer to your actual opening, not just the plan's numbers — measure the interior width once the divider and sides are in, and keep the drawer box about 1" narrower than that opening so the slides have the clearance they need. Attach the drawer face with a couple of screws in addition to the nails and glue; nails alone are enough to hold at first but a drawer face gets grabbed and pulled every single day, and that repeated stress is exactly when a nail-only joint tends to let go.

steps.title: Attach the sides to the footers
steps.body: Attach the sides to the bottom footer pieces with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, hidden on the inside faces.

steps.title: Attach the bottom shelf
steps.body: Set the bottom shelf on the footer pieces and attach — pocket holes work well, and reinforcing with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue into the footers adds extra hold.

steps.title: Add the divider
steps.body: Build the divider panel separately with pocket holes, position it inside the cabinet, then attach to the side with glue and finish nails.

steps.title: Add the top rails
steps.body: Attach the 1x2 top rails across the upper portion of the cabinet with pocket holes — these tie the case together and give a shared desk countertop something to screw into later.

steps.title: Build and install the drawer
steps.body: Measure the actual interior opening for the drawer, and build the drawer box about 1" narrower than that measurement. Install it into the base with the drawer slides, keeping a 3/4" gap at the front to allow room for the drawer face.

steps.title: Attach the drawer face
steps.body: Attach the drawer face with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue, then add a couple of screws from inside the drawer box for extra hold — a face that's grabbed daily benefits from more than nails alone. If you're installing hardware, it's easier to attach the knob or handle before mounting the face to the drawer.

images.alt: A short plywood desk-system base cabinet with a drawer and open cubby, sized for a computer tower
